data.description:
## Why

Completion-verification is single-pass and, by contract, emits findings ONLY for FAILING criteria
(`reconcile_verdict` flips a PASS-with-findings to FAIL). On a PASS it persists just signed counts
via the attestation — no per-criterion record exists anywhere, and the FAIL-only sidecar never
fires. So a successfully-closed ticket has no durable per-criterion completion audit, unlike its
plan and code reviews.

## What

Give completion-verification a lossless PASS-path audit record:
1. Extend the verifier's output contract so, in addition to failure findings, it returns a positive
   per-criterion evaluation array: one entry per acceptance/success criterion evaluated, each with
   its met status, a source-code (or attestation) citation, and its
   codebase-verifiable / operator-attested kind.
2. Emit a completion sidecar on PASS (not only FAIL) persisting that array plus the verdict, runner,
   model, and material fingerprint.

Completion stays single-pass — this adds a per-criterion result list, NOT a four-pass structure.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json` — add an OPTIONAL `criteria[]` array
  (`{criterion, met, citation, kind}`); PASS stays `findings == []` (positive results live in
  `criteria[]`, so the failures-only findings invariant is preserved).
- `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` — thread `criteria[]` through `reconcile_verdict` (a populated
  `criteria[]` must NOT trip the PASS-with-findings→FAIL flip); update the verifier prompt to emit it.
- `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` — emit on PASS as well as FAIL; bump schema; add a
  `latest_pass_record` reader.

## Approach rationale

Add a separate OPTIONAL `criteria[]` array rather than repurposing `findings` so the strong
failures-only `findings` invariant (and every existing consumer of it) is untouched; positive
results are a new, additive channel. This keeps the change back-compatible and the invariant intact.

## Compatibility & rollback

The `criteria[]` field is OPTIONAL — an older agent/response that omits it yields today's behavior
(PASS with counts only) and the failures-only findings invariant is unchanged, so nothing regresses.
No backfill. Reverting stops the PASS sidecar and drops the field; existing records stay readable.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A completion PASS returns a `criteria[]` array with one met-record per evaluated criterion, and
      `findings` stays empty. Oracle: a unit test on `reconcile_verdict` feeding a PASS verdict with a
      populated `criteria[]`, asserting it is NOT flipped to FAIL and the array survives.
- [ ] A completion PASS emits a completion sidecar event persisting the per-criterion array. Oracle:
      an integration test driving the verifier with a stub agent returning the new shape and asserting
      on the emitted event JSON.
- [ ] Live end-to-end: running the real completion verifier (`[agents]` extra + API key) on a fixture
      ticket whose criteria are met produces a PASS with a populated `criteria[]` persisted to the
      sidecar. Oracle: a live/E2E test that exercises the real agent path, gated on LLM availability
      (skipped with a recorded reason otherwise) — the cutover live-exercise DoD for this new path.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
data.title: completion-verification lossless PASS capture (per-criterion record)
